name: Academic Research Assistant — PhD Student
description: AI research assistant supporting a PhD student in computational biology over 6 months. Tracks thesis evolution, literature findings, methodology changes, and advisor feedback.

turns.content: I'm Priya, PhD student in computational biology at UMich, third year. My advisor is Dr. Kwon.
Context: Priya, 3rd-year CompBio PhD, University of Michigan, advisor Dr. Kwon.
My thesis is on predicting protein-protein interaction networks from single-cell RNA-seq data using graph neural networks.
Thesis: PPI network prediction from scRNA-seq using GNNs. Frontier intersection of three fields.
I'm using PyTorch Geometric and Scanpy as my main tools. Running experiments on a 40-GPU cluster at UMich.
Tech stack: PyTorch Geometric, Scanpy. Compute: UMich 40-GPU cluster.
My main dataset is the Human Cell Atlas — about 1.2M cells across 33 tissues.
Primary dataset: Human Cell Atlas, 1.2M cells, 33 tissues.
Found a key paper: Yao et al. 2023 in Nature Methods — they use a graph autoencoder for PPI prediction but only on bulk RNA-seq. My novelty is the single-cell resolution.
Key related work: Yao et al. 2023 (Nature Methods), graph autoencoder for PPI from bulk RNA-seq. Priya's novelty: single-cell resolution.
I initially planned to use a vanilla GCN but Dr. Kwon suggested Graph Transformers might capture long-range dependencies better. I'm switching.
Architecture decision: vanilla GCN dropped, switching to Graph Transformers per Dr. Kwon's advice for better long-range dependency capture.
Dr. Kwon also wants me to benchmark against STRING database predictions. Adding that to the eval plan.
Eval plan updated: add STRING database benchmark as a comparison baseline.
First experiments done. Graph Transformer is overfitting badly — validation AUC is 0.61 while training AUC hits 0.97. Not good.
Overfitting issue: Graph Transformer training AUC 0.97, validation AUC 0.61. Large gap — classic overfit.
Dr. Kwon reviewed and said the model complexity is too high for the number of labeled PPI edges we have. He wants me to try a simpler GraphSAGE approach with dropout.
Architecture change: Graph Transformer → GraphSAGE with dropout. Reason: too complex for labeled data volume.
Also found a problem in my preprocessing — I was using raw counts instead of normalized log1p counts. Fixed. This changes all prior results.
CRITICAL bug: raw counts used instead of log1p normalized. Fixed. All prior experiments are invalid — need rerun.
After the normalization fix and switching to GraphSAGE: validation AUC is 0.84. Much better.
Results improved significantly: validation AUC 0.84 with GraphSAGE + correct normalization.
We also outperform STRING baseline by 7 points on the held-out tissue panel. Dr. Kwon is happy.
Benchmark: +7% AUC over STRING baseline. Dr. Kwon satisfied with progress.
My thesis chapter 2 draft is done. Dr. Kwon wants me to add a section on computational complexity — he thinks reviewers will ask about scalability.
Chapter 2 draft complete. Action: add computational complexity/scalability section per Dr. Kwon.
Got access to a new dataset — Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells across the nervous system. This is huge.
New dataset: Allen Brain Cell Atlas, 3.4M cells, nervous system. Nearly 3x the primary dataset size.
Dr. Kwon wants to expand the thesis to include a neural-tissue-specific PPI analysis. This adds a third thesis chapter.
Thesis scope expanded: new Chapter 3 on neural-tissue PPI. Thesis now three chapters instead of two.
I switched from PyTorch Geometric to DGL — better scaling for the Allen data. Scanpy stays.
Tech stack update: PyTorch Geometric → DGL (better scaling). Scanpy unchanged.
Submitting a paper to NeurIPS workshop on computational biology. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.'
Paper submission: NeurIPS CompBio workshop. Title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.'
Dr. Kwon set the thesis defense date for May of next year. That's about 11 months away.
Defense date: May next year. ~11 months out.
The normalization bug I fixed earlier — I'm documenting it as a key methods contribution. The correct log1p treatment turns out to matter a lot for downstream GNN stability.
Methods contribution: log1p normalization importance for GNN stability documented as a finding. What started as a bug became a validated insight.

queries.query: What neural network architecture is Priya currently using?
queries.expected_answer: GraphSAGE with dropout. Started with GCN (original plan), switched to Graph Transformer (Dr. Kwon's suggestion), then switched again to GraphSAGE due to overfitting.
queries.trap: Architecture changed three times. Must return the final architecture, not GCN or Graph Transformer.

queries.query: What is the current primary deep learning library being used?
queries.expected_answer: DGL (Deep Graph Library). Replaced PyTorch Geometric in session 5 for better scaling with the Allen Brain dataset.
queries.trap: PyTorch Geometric established in session 1. Session 5 replaces it with DGL. Scanpy unchanged.

queries.query: What validation AUC did the first Graph Transformer experiments achieve?
queries.expected_answer: 0.61 validation AUC (with 0.97 training AUC — severe overfitting).
queries.trap: Current model achieves 0.84. Must return the historical value for the Graph Transformer specifically.

queries.query: What preprocessing bug was found and what was the impact?
queries.expected_answer: Raw counts were used instead of log1p normalized counts. All prior experiments were invalid and had to be rerun. The fix also turned out to be a key methods contribution — log1p normalization critically affects GNN stability.
queries.trap: Session 3 flags the bug. Session 6 recasts it as a contribution. Must connect both sessions for the full answer.

queries.query: What are both datasets Priya has access to?
queries.expected_answer: Human Cell Atlas (1.2M cells, 33 tissues — primary) and Allen Brain Cell Atlas (3.4M cells, nervous system — added in session 5).
queries.trap: Must list both datasets, not just the original one.

queries.query: How many chapters does the thesis have now?
queries.expected_answer: Three. A third chapter on neural-tissue PPI was added when the Allen Brain dataset was incorporated.
queries.trap: Session 4 references Chapter 2, implying two chapters. Session 5 adds a third.

queries.query: What is Priya's current validation AUC?
queries.expected_answer: 0.84 — after switching to GraphSAGE and fixing the normalization bug.
queries.trap: Previous AUC was 0.61. Must return the improved, current figure.

queries.query: Why did Dr. Kwon suggest switching away from Graph Transformers?
queries.expected_answer: Model complexity was too high for the number of labeled PPI edges in the dataset, causing severe overfitting.

queries.query: What conference is Priya submitting a paper to?
queries.expected_answer: NeurIPS Computational Biology workshop. Paper title: 'Single-cell GNN for PPI Network Inference at Tissue Resolution.'

queries.query: Who is Priya's thesis advisor?
queries.expected_answer: Dr. Kwon.

queries.query: What is the key related work paper, and how does Priya's thesis differ from it?
queries.expected_answer: Yao et al. 2023 (Nature Methods) — graph autoencoder for PPI prediction from bulk RNA-seq. Priya's novelty: single-cell resolution using scRNA-seq, enabling tissue-specific PPI inference.

queries.query: What feedback did Dr. Kwon give on Chapter 2?
queries.expected_answer: Add a section on computational complexity and scalability — anticipating reviewer questions.

queries.query: By how much does the model outperform the STRING database baseline?
queries.expected_answer: 7 percentage points AUC on the held-out tissue panel.

queries.query: When is Priya's thesis defense?
queries.expected_answer: May of next year (~11 months away from when the defense date was set).

queries.query: What is the original thesis architecture that was abandoned?
queries.expected_answer: Vanilla GCN — the original plan before Dr. Kwon suggested Graph Transformers.
queries.trap: Two architectures were abandoned (GCN and Graph Transformer). The question asks for the original — which is GCN.

queries.query: Is PyTorch Geometric still in use?
queries.expected_answer: No. Replaced by DGL in session 5 for better scaling with the Allen Brain dataset.
queries.trap: PyTorch Geometric established in session 1. Session 5 replaces it. Naive systems say yes.

queries.query: What was the training vs. validation AUC gap during the overfitting phase?
queries.expected_answer: Training AUC 0.97, validation AUC 0.61 — a gap of 0.36, indicating severe overfitting.
queries.trap: Requires recalling both values and computing the delta.

queries.query: What is the original dataset size compared to the new one?
queries.expected_answer: Human Cell Atlas: 1.2M cells. Allen Brain Cell Atlas: 3.4M cells — about 2.8x larger.
queries.trap: Requires recalling both dataset sizes and computing the relative scale.

queries.query: What was added to the evaluation plan per Dr. Kwon?
queries.expected_answer: STRING database benchmark as a comparison baseline.

queries.query: Describe the full architecture evolution from start to current.
queries.expected_answer: 1) Started with vanilla GCN (original plan). 2) Switched to Graph Transformer (Dr. Kwon's suggestion for long-range dependencies). 3) Graph Transformer overfit severely (train AUC 0.97, val 0.61). 4) Switched to GraphSAGE with dropout (Dr. Kwon: too complex for labeled data). 5) Also fixed log1p normalization bug — reran all experiments. 6) GraphSAGE achieved validation AUC 0.84.
queries.trap: Requires reconstructing the full evolution in order across sessions 1-4, including the bug fix that invalidated intermediate results.
